mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous housing market contraction and persistent inflation compress consumer purchasing power, leading to weaker corporate revenue growth and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over a 3-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy aggregate supply, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are flat or negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below a 1.5% annualized threshold indicates an economic slowdown that erodes corporate earnings expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The market rallies despite GDP growth remaining below 1.5% for two consecutive quarters, or the signal's information coefficient remains statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Tight monetary policy combined with robust employment growth creates a liquidity squeeze that forces leveraged equity positions to unwind, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both rates are rising and payrolls exceed 200k monthly, or the interaction effect disappears after controlling for volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in retail sales and consumer spending directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in spending-up periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 4.5% paired with a sharp decline in housing starts signals a deepening economic contraction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the combined signal fails to reject the null hypothesis of no predictive power in a 5-year out-of-sample test.
